surface_verification.justification: No runtime surface exists to exercise. Three of the four touched files are test-harness code (probe dispatch table, two test modules); the fourth, shared/scripts/lib/fr_table_reader.py, gains a first-party-name guard plus comments and no parsing behaviour change — proven by its five consumers staying green across all three import realms (compliance 1295, shared 4728, integration 416). No API route, store mutation, SSE/WS handler, message contract, or UI-consumed code is touched. The change's actual output surface is the SCANNER, which is verified directly: semgrep reports 3 findings before and 0 after, repo-wide.

behaviors.behavior: probe dispatch resolves each name to its function without indexing globals()
behaviors.evidence: integration-tests/test_requirements_corpus_false_verdicts.py + _found_defects.py — 7 probe names driven end-to-end through the subprocess runner, 20 passed

behaviors.behavior: every probe-table row maps to the correspondingly-named function
behaviors.evidence: integration-tests/test_requirements_corpus_probe_table.py::test_every_probe_table_row_resolves_to_its_like_named_function PASSED; mutation-verified ('t1': probe_t2 makes it RED)

behaviors.behavior: every module-level probe_* function is registered in the table (incl. never-invoked t2)
--probe accepts exactly the eight documented names
the adopt generator still emits the canonical FR-table header after the -c program was hoisted out of the argv list
a reintroduced pytest.skip inside pre_s6_sections fails the shallow-clone meta-test hard instead of skipping it
behaviors.evidence: integration-tests/test_fr_history_skip_guard.py::test_a_reintroduced_skip_makes_the_meta_test_fail_not_skip PASSED — runs the real meta-test in a child pytest with pre_s6_sections patched to skip; asserts '1 failed' and NOT '1 skipped'

behaviors.behavior: a non-Skipped exception from pre_s6_sections propagates instead of being converted to pytest.fail
behaviors.evidence: The AssertionError path is exercised by the meta-test itself and the Skipped path by test_fr_history_skip_guard.py; what remains is the ABSENCE of a handler (Python's default propagation), so no branch of this diff is left unexercised.

behaviors.behavior: _ALLOWED_SIBLINGS equals the set of siblings actually imported (no stale entry widening the guard)
behaviors.evidence: integration-tests/test_fr_table_reader_load_styles.py::test_the_declared_sibling_allowlist_matches_what_is_actually_loaded[4 load styles] PASSED; mutation-verified (an unused 'os' entry reddens all four)

behaviors.behavior: an undeclared sibling name is refused before reaching importlib.import_module
behaviors.evidence: integration-tests/test_fr_table_reader_load_styles.py::test_an_undeclared_sibling_name_is_refused_before_it_reaches_import[4 load styles] PASSED; mutation-verified; plus test_fr_table_reader_contract.py::test_an_undeclared_sibling_is_refused in-process — the subprocess assertions are invisible to coverage, which the diff-coverage gate caught (the raise showed as an uncovered changed line)

behaviors.behavior: Semgrep reports none of non-literal-import / dangerous-globals-use anywhere in the repo
behaviors.evidence: semgrep 1.170.0 --config r/python.lang.security.audit.non-literal-import --config r/python.lang.security.dangerous-globals-use over the repo: 3 findings BEFORE the diff (alerts 1257/1258/1260 at their exact reported lines), 0 AFTER

behaviors.behavior: the new _sibling guard breaks no consumer in any import realm
behaviors.evidence: shared/tests 4728 passed + integration-tests 416 passed + plugins/shipwright-compliance 1295 passed, all green
